description: VPC Flow Logs not enabled
details: Without Flow Logs there is no network-level audit trail. Lateral movement, data exfiltration over unusual ports, and reconnaissance scans inside the VPC are invisible to defenders.
mitigations: Enable VPC Flow Logs (ACCEPT, REJECT, or ALL) to CloudWatch Logs or S3. Set retention to meet your compliance requirement. Consider enabling flow logs at the subnet level for finer granularity.

description: Production workloads deployed in the default VPC
details: The default VPC has permissive defaults (all subnets public, default security group allows all intra-VPC traffic) and is shared across all AWS services in the account. Deploying production workloads here increases blast radius and makes least-privilege networking harder to achieve.
mitigations: Create a dedicated VPC with private subnets for production workloads. Delete or restrict the default VPC using an SCP to prevent accidental use.

description: VPC peering without PrivateLink — transitive routing risk
details: VPC peering is non-transitive, but a hub VPC with multiple peering connections and overly broad route tables can inadvertently allow cross-peering traffic. PrivateLink is the recommended alternative for service exposure.
condition: target.oneOf(AWSVPC) and target.peeringEnabled and not target.privateLink
mitigations: Prefer VPC endpoints (PrivateLink) over peering for service-to-service communication. If peering is required, audit route tables to ensure only the minimum required CIDR ranges are routed across each peering connection.

description: Public subnet auto-assigns public IP addresses at launch
details: When mapPublicIpOnLaunch is enabled, any instance launched in the subnet receives a public IP automatically — including instances that were intended to be private. A single misconfigured launch configuration can expose an instance directly to the internet.
mitigations: Disable auto-assign public IP on all subnets. Assign Elastic IPs explicitly only where needed (e.g. NAT Gateways). Use the ALB or CloudFront as the internet-facing entry point instead.

description: Private subnet without custom Network ACL
details: The default Network ACL allows all inbound and outbound traffic. Without a custom NACL on private subnets, there is no stateless subnet-level enforcement to complement security groups — leaving no defence-in-depth against a compromised host in the same subnet.
condition: target.oneOf(AWSSubnet) and not target.isPublic and not target.hasNaclIngress
mitigations: Define custom NACLs for private subnets that deny traffic from unexpected source CIDRs and restrict egress to known destinations. Treat NACLs as a coarse second layer — security groups remain the primary control.

description: EC2 instance metadata service accessible without IMDSv2
details: IMDSv1 allows SSRF attacks to retrieve instance credentials via a simple HTTP GET. IMDSv2 requires a session-oriented PUT request that prevents most SSRF exploits.
mitigations: Enforce IMDSv2 by setting HttpTokens=required on the instance metadata service. Apply via instance metadata options or through an SCP.

description: EC2 instance publicly accessible with overly permissive security groups
details: An instance that is publicly accessible and has security groups open to broad IP ranges increases the attack surface for exploitation of exposed services.
mitigations: Restrict security group rules to known CIDR ranges. Use a bastion host or AWS Systems Manager Session Manager instead of exposing SSH/RDP.

description: EC2 instance without an IAM role relying on long-lived credentials
details: Without an IAM instance profile, applications typically fall back to baked-in access keys, which cannot be automatically rotated and are frequently leaked.
mitigations: Attach an IAM instance profile with least-privilege permissions. Use IAM roles instead of embedding access keys in the instance or its user data.

description: EBS volumes not encrypted at rest
details: Unencrypted EBS volumes expose data if snapshots are shared accidentally or if physical storage is compromised.
mitigations: Enable EBS default encryption at the account level. Encrypt all volumes at launch using a customer-managed KMS key.

description: S3 bucket publicly readable
details: A publicly readable bucket exposes all objects to unauthenticated internet access, commonly leading to sensitive data leaks.
mitigations: Enable S3 Block Public Access at both the bucket and account level. Review and tighten bucket ACLs and bucket policies.

description: S3 bucket publicly writable
details: A publicly writable bucket allows arbitrary actors to upload objects, enabling data exfiltration hosting, malware distribution, or cost amplification attacks.
mitigations: Remove all public write ACLs and bucket policy statements. Enable S3 Block Public Access.

description: S3 bucket storing sensitive data without encryption
details: S3 objects containing PII or sensitive data stored without SSE are readable in plaintext if bucket access controls are misconfigured.
condition: target.oneOf(S3Bucket) and target.storesSensitiveData and not target.encrypted
mitigations: Enable SSE-S3 or SSE-KMS with a default bucket encryption policy. Use a bucket policy to deny unencrypted PutObject requests.

description: S3 bucket without versioning storing sensitive data
details: Without versioning, data is permanently lost on accidental delete or ransomware overwrite. There is no recovery path.
condition: target.oneOf(S3Bucket) and target.storesSensitiveData and not target.versioned
mitigations: Enable S3 versioning and configure Object Lock or MFA delete for critical buckets.

description: RDS instance publicly accessible
details: A publicly accessible RDS endpoint exposes the database to brute-force and exploitation attempts from the internet.
mitigations: Set PubliclyAccessible=false. Place the instance in a private subnet and access it via a bastion host, VPN, or AWS PrivateLink.

description: RDS instance storage not encrypted
details: Unencrypted RDS storage exposes database files, transaction logs, and backups if the underlying media is compromised.
mitigations: Enable storage encryption at instance creation using a KMS key. Note: encryption cannot be enabled on a running instance — a snapshot restore with encryption is required.

description: RDS instance without deletion protection
details: Without deletion protection, a misconfigured IaC pipeline or a compromised account can permanently destroy the database.
mitigations: Enable DeletionProtection on all production RDS instances. Combine with automated backups and Multi-AZ.

description: RDS not using IAM authentication, relying on password credentials
details: Password-based database authentication requires rotating credentials manually and risks credential stuffing or leakage via environment variables.
mitigations: Enable IAM database authentication and grant db-connect IAM permissions to application roles instead of distributing DB passwords.

description: Lambda function not deployed in a VPC
details: A Lambda outside a VPC accesses downstream resources over the public internet, bypassing network-layer controls such as VPC security groups and NACLs.
mitigations: Deploy the Lambda into a private VPC subnet. Use VPC endpoints for AWS service access to avoid public internet egress.

description: Lambda function without code signing
details: Without code signing, a compromised CI/CD pipeline or S3 deployment bucket can silently replace function code with malicious versions.
mitigations: Enable a Lambda code signing configuration that references a trusted AWS Signer profile. Set the policy to Enforce to reject unsigned deployments.

description: Lambda function uses environment variables for secrets instead of Secrets Manager
details: Secrets in plaintext environment variables are visible in the Lambda console, CloudTrail logs, and to anyone with GetFunctionConfiguration permissions.
condition: target.oneOf(LambdaFunction) and target.usesEnvironmentVariables and not target.hasSecretsManagerIntegration
mitigations: Store secrets in AWS Secrets Manager or SSM Parameter Store (SecureString). Fetch them at runtime using the Lambda extension or SDK, not via environment variables.

description: API Gateway exposed without an authorizer
details: An API Gateway stage with no Lambda authorizer, Cognito User Pool authorizer, or IAM authorization allows unauthenticated calls to backend resources.
condition: target.oneOf(APIGateway) and not target.hasAuthorizer and not target.isPrivate
mitigations: Attach a Cognito User Pool or Lambda authorizer to all routes. For internal APIs, use IAM authorization or make the API private.

description: API Gateway without WAF protection
details: Without AWS WAF, the API is vulnerable to common web exploits (SQLi, XSS), bot traffic, and HTTP flood attacks.
condition: target.oneOf(APIGateway) and not target.hasWAF and not target.isPrivate
mitigations: Associate an AWS WAF Web ACL with the API Gateway stage. Enable managed rule groups for SQL injection, known bad inputs, and rate-based rules.

description: API Gateway without access logging
details: Without access logs, there is no audit trail of API calls, making it impossible to detect unauthorized access or investigate incidents.
mitigations: Enable access logging for the stage to a CloudWatch Logs group. Configure log retention and ship logs to a SIEM.

description: IAM role with administrator access
details: A role with AdministratorAccess or equivalent wildcard permissions violates least privilege and provides full account compromise if the role is assumed by an attacker.
mitigations: Scope role permissions to the minimum required actions and resources. Use permission boundaries and SCPs to enforce ceiling permissions.

description: IAM role with cross-account trust without conditions
details: A trust policy allowing cross-account assumption without condition keys (e.g. sts:ExternalId or aws:PrincipalOrgID) is vulnerable to the confused deputy problem.
condition: target.oneOf(IAMRole) and target.crossAccountTrust and not target.hasConditions
mitigations: Add an sts:ExternalId condition to the trust policy for third-party roles. Use aws:PrincipalOrgID to restrict assumption to accounts within your AWS Organization.

description: IAM role with inline policies instead of managed policies
details: Inline policies are harder to audit, cannot be reused, and are not tracked individually in CloudTrail, making least-privilege reviews error-prone.
mitigations: Convert inline policies to customer-managed policies. Use AWS IAM Access Analyzer to validate policy correctness and scope.

description: SQS queue without server-side encryption
details: Messages in transit between SQS and consumers are protected by TLS, but messages at rest in the queue are stored in plaintext without SSE.
mitigations: Enable SSE-SQS (free) or SSE-KMS with a CMK for stricter access control and auditability.

description: SQS queue publicly accessible
details: A queue with a resource policy that allows public access (Principal: *) can receive or expose messages to arbitrary principals.
mitigations: Remove wildcard principal statements from the queue policy. Restrict access to specific IAM principals or VPC endpoints.

description: CloudFront distribution allows HTTP (unencrypted) viewer connections
details: Permitting HTTP allows traffic to be intercepted in transit. Sensitive cookies or tokens transmitted over HTTP are exposed to network eavesdroppers.
mitigations: Set the viewer protocol policy to 'Redirect HTTP to HTTPS' or 'HTTPS Only' on all cache behaviours.

description: CloudFront distribution without WAF protection
details: Without AWS WAF, the distribution has no layer-7 protection against common web exploits, scrapers, or volumetric HTTP floods.
mitigations: Associate an AWS WAF Web ACL with the distribution. Enable managed rule groups for common threats and configure rate-based rules.

description: CloudFront S3 origin not protected by Origin Access Control
details: Without OAC, the S3 bucket must have its own public access policy or bucket ACL, making it independently addressable and potentially exposing objects that bypass CloudFront controls.
mitigations: Create an Origin Access Control (OAC) and attach it to the CloudFront origin. Update the S3 bucket policy to allow access only from the CloudFront service principal.

description: ALB does not redirect HTTP to HTTPS
details: An ALB listener accepting HTTP without a redirect rule allows clients to send credentials or session tokens in plaintext.
condition: target.oneOf(ALB) and not target.httpsOnly and not target.isInternal
mitigations: Add an HTTP listener rule that returns a 301 redirect to the HTTPS listener. Remove the HTTP forwarding rule.

description: ALB access logging disabled
details: Without access logs, there is no record of requests reaching the load balancer — making forensic investigation of incidents impossible.
mitigations: Enable ALB access logging to an S3 bucket. Ensure the bucket policy grants the Elastic Load Balancing service account write access.

description: ALB not dropping invalid HTTP headers
details: Forwarding non-standard or malformed HTTP headers to backend instances can facilitate HTTP request smuggling attacks.
mitigations: Enable the 'routing.http.drop_invalid_header_fields.enabled' attribute on the ALB.

description: ElastiCache cluster without encryption in transit or at rest
details: Cache data transmitted or stored in plaintext can be intercepted within the VPC or read if a snapshot is exposed.
mitigations: Enable TLS for in-transit encryption and enable encryption at rest. For Redis, use a TLS-capable client and set --transit-encryption-enabled.

description: EFS file system not encrypted at rest
details: An unencrypted EFS file system exposes all stored data (including WordPress core files, plugins, and uploaded media) if the underlying storage is compromised or a mount target is misconfigured.
mitigations: Enable EFS encryption at rest using an AWS KMS key at file system creation time. Encryption cannot be enabled on an existing unencrypted file system.

description: EFS NFS connections not enforced to use TLS
details: Without in-transit encryption, NFS traffic between EC2 instances and EFS mount targets is transmitted in plaintext within the VPC, exposing file contents to any host that can intercept VPC traffic.
mitigations: Mount EFS using the amazon-efs-utils helper with the 'tls' mount option (efs-utils enforces TLS via stunnel). Deny unencrypted NFS connections using a file system policy with the 'elasticfilesystem:clientRootAccess' or 'aws:SecureTransport' condition.

description: ElastiCache Redis cluster without AUTH token
details: Without an AUTH token, any host that can reach the Redis endpoint (e.g. a compromised workload in the same VPC) can read or overwrite cache data without authentication.
condition: target.oneOf(ElastiCacheCluster) and target.engine == 'redis' and not target.authEnabled
mitigations: Set an AUTH token at cluster creation and rotate it using the ModifyReplicationGroup API. Combine with VPC security groups to limit endpoint access.
